post.hook: Your CI pipeline is a credential vault. Attackers know it.
post.body:
CI/CD pipelines hold secrets, build artifacts, and deployment credentials. A single compromised dependency or poisoned build step can cascade through your entire software supply chain. This package covers 9 supply chain attack techniques end-to-end.

What's inside the Supply Chain Package Compromise Defense SOaC package:
→ 0 detection rules (multi-platform)
→ 0 SOAR playbooks
→ 0 policy & runbook documents
→ 0 validated replay scenarios
→ MITRE ATT&CK: T1195.002, T1204.005, T1027, T1071.001, T1105, T1036.005, T1547.001, T1219, T1070.004

Every detection in this Advanced-tier package has been validated against real attack telemetry in Supply Chain, CI/CD, Network, Endpoint. The included SOAR playbooks integrate with your existing stack — no vendor lock-in, no proprietary formats. Detections ship as code. Playbooks ship as code. Policies ship as code.
post.cta: 💬 Comment “CICD” and I’ll DM you the full package.

engagement.first_comment:
🔗 Full package on GitHub: https://github.com/ge0mant1s/SOaC-Enterprise/tree/main/packages/014_supply_chain_package_compromise_defense

One detail not in the post: the 0 detection rules include cross-platform correlation logic — a single alert fires when activity matches across multiple telemetry sources, reducing false positives by focusing on attack chains rather than isolated events.
engagement.poll_question: Does your org scan CI artifacts and verify build provenance before deployment?
engagement.follow_up_day3: 48 hours in — people asked for this. Here's one thing the package catches that most SIEMs miss: build artifact hash mutation detection — catches tag poisoning before compromised packages reach production.
